surface: issuer raising via a registered funding portal
note: Control framework for the funding-portal surface, driven by the issuer's Form C (driver: form_c). Demonstrates the multi-sovereign authority model (docs/authority-model.md): a Reg CF raise lives under Federal-SEC (Reg CF, 17 CFR Part 227) + FINRA (Funding Portal Rules, binding on the portal) + money-transmission (who holds investor funds) + state-blue-sky simultaneously. Form C supplies the public-side legs (Form C on file, offering cap vs the $5M rolling ceiling, the single named intermediary by CIK/CRD/007- file number, signature persons for the 227.503 bad-actor screen); portal_finra_member is auto-verified against the SEC (EDGAR CFPORTAL) + FINRA registers; funds_qualified_third_party (money-transmission) is evidenced from the portal's Form Funding Portal escrow disclosure + the custodian's broker-dealer registration (X-17A-5); portal_prohibited_conduct reuses that same Form Funding Portal for the funds-handling prong + the portal's criminal/regulatory/civil disclosures (FP Rule 200), surfacing compensation WITHOUT opining on it (a portal may lawfully take transaction-based comp); cf_single_intermediary cross-checks the issuer's own Form C filings for a single named intermediary (distinct intermediaries over overlapping offering windows escalate; sequential raises are permitted). The remaining advice/solicitation conduct, revenue-report, and state legs remain private/runnable and resolve to open/escalate. Template for counsel sign-off, not legal advice.

controls.obligation: Issuer raises no more than $5,000,000 via Reg CF in a rolling 12-month period.
Form C filed with the SEC (EDGAR) before the offering commences; Form C-AR filed annually.
The offering is conducted exclusively through ONE SEC-registered intermediary (funding portal or broker-dealer).
No covered person is subject to a Reg CF bad-actor disqualifying event.
The intermediary is a registered funding portal (Form Funding Portal) AND a FINRA member.
The portal does not give advice/recommendations, solicit, hold/manage investor funds, or take transaction-based compensation.
The portal files Form FP-Statement of Revenue within 60 days of fiscal year-end.
Investor funds are held by a qualified third party (bank/escrow), not the portal; AML/BSA controls applied where applicable.
Confirm state treatment: Reg CF securities are covered securities (state registration preempted); satisfy any residual notice + state antifraud.
